rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAUREEN E. CROWE TRUST DATED JULY 16, 2016
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Gregory Crowe To: Response To Request For Production Of Documents; Further Resonse To Request For Production Of Documents; Further Produce Documents Ansd Provide A Complete And Legally Proper Privilege Log Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: [3 of 3] When asserting claims of privilege or attorney work product protection, the objecting party must provide "sufficient factual information" to enable other parties to evaluate the merits of the claim, "including, if necessary, a privilege log." CCP Section 2031.240(c)(1); Lopez v. Watchtower Bible & Tract Soc. of New York, Inc. (2016) 246 Cal.App.4th 566, 596-597 (burden to show preliminary facts supporting application of privilege not met where D failed to produce privilege log or identify any specific confidential communications); see also CCP Section 2031.240(c)(2). The purpose of a privilege log is to make a record of the documents withheld and the privilege claim asserted as to each. Wellpoint Health Networks, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (McCombs) (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 110, 130; Best Products, Inc. v. Sup. Ct. (Granatelli Motorsports, Inc.) (2004) 119 Cal.App.4th 1181, 1188-1189. As the term is commonly used by courts and attorneys, a "privilege log" identifies each document for which a privilege or work product protection is claimed, its author, recipients, date of preparation, and the specific privilege or work product protection claimed. Hernandez v. Sup. Ct. (Acheson Indus., Inc.) (2003) 112 Cal.app.4th 285, 291-292, fn. 6; see CCP Section 2031.240(c)(2). The privilege log provided by Crowe, including the supplemental privilege log included with the opposition, does not adequately identity each document Crowe claims is privileged and the factual basis for the privilege as required under Catalina Island Yacht Club v. Superior Court (2015) 242 Cal. App.4th 1116, 1127. The privilege log provided does not include the capacity of all individuals who authored, sent, or received each allegedly privileged document (some of the recipients in the "TO" column have been cut off) and does not provide a brief description of the document and its contents or subject matter sufficient to determine whether the privilege applies (the short "SUBJECT" column does not provide this information). In short, the privilege log provided does not adequately identify each document and the factual basis for the privilege claim. Respondent Crowe shall prepare and lodge with the court clerk, attention Probate Law and Motion, a hard-copy proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling prior to the hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is available in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAUREEN E. CROWE TRUST DATED JULY 16, 2016
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Gregory Crowe To: Response To Request For Production Of Documents;Further Response To Request For Production Of Documents; Further Produce Documents;Andprovide A Complete And Legally Proper Privilege Log (Added For Tentative Ruling Entry Purposes Only)
rulings.Rulings: [1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody. Respondent Crowe's request for judicial notice located inside his memorandum of points and authorities is DENIED. "Any request for judicial notice must be made in a separate document listing the specific items for which notice is requested . . ." CRC 3.113(l). Petitioners' Motion to Compel Gregory Crowe to: Response to Request for Production of Documents; Further Response to Request for Production of Documents; Further Produce Documents; and Provide a Complete and Legally Proper Privilege Log is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. As an initial matter, the motion is procedurally defective because the notice of motion does not include the grounds for the motion pursuant to CCP Section 1010 and CRC 3.110. Furthermore, the points and authorities is impermissibly long at seventeen pages. CRC 3.113(d). Petitioners could have applied to file a longer points and authorities pursuant to CRC 3.113(e), but they did not do so. For his part Respondent Crowe filed and served his opposition late - six days before the hearing as opposed to nine days before the hearing as required by CCP Section 1005(b). Pursuant to CRC 3.1300(d), the Court may refuse to consider the memorandum of points and authorities and the opposition due to the deficiencies; however, the Court chooses to exercise its discretion to excuse both deficiencies and consider the motion on its merits. Going forward, however, the parties will be expected to satisfy all requirements for discovery motions, including meaningful good faith meet and confer attempts. Further waivers of procedural infirmities are not contemplated. The Motion to Compel Gregory Crowe to: Response to Request for Production of Documents is DENIED.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MAUREEN E. CROWE TRUST DATED JULY 16, 2016
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Gregory Crowe To: Response To Request For Production Of Documents;Further Response To Request For Production Of Documents; Further Produce Documents;Andprovide A Complete And Legally Proper Privilege Log (Added For Tentative Ruling Entry Puruposes Only)
rulings.Rulings: [2 of 3] Where no response at all has been made, the motion is to compel a response pursuant to CCP Section 2031.300. The allegation that Crowe failed to respond to the request for production as an individual is without merit. Crowe, as Beneficiary and Trustee of the Lia Fail 2004 Revocable Trust dated December 10, 2004, provided responses to the discovery. "Beneficiary" means a person to whom a donative transfer of property is made or that person's successor in interest, and . . .(c) As it relates to a trust, means a person who has any present or future interest, vested or contingent." Probate Code Section 24. ""Person" means an individual . . ." Probate Code Section 56. An individual and a beneficiary are one and the same. Crowe made responses as a beneficiary, therefore, he also made responses as an individual. Where a response has been made, but the demanding party is not satisfied with it, the remedy is a motion to compel FURTHER responses, not a motion to compel response. CCP Section 2031.310. The Motion to Compel Gregory Crowe to: Further Response to Request for Production of Documents; Further Produce Documents is GRANTED as to objections based on attorney-client privilege and DENIED as to all other objections. A complete privilege log sufficiently specific to allow a determination of whether each withheld document is or is not in fact privileged must be provided as discussed below within twenty days of this order. Where responses have been made but they are not satisfactory to the demanding party, the motion is to compel further responses pursuant to CCP Section 2031.310. A motion to compel further Response for Production of Documents "shall set forth specific facts showing good cause justifying the discovery sought by the demand." CCP 2031.310(b)(1); Kirkland v. Sup. Ct. (Guess? Inc.) (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 92, 98. To show good cause, the burden is on the moving party to show both: (1) relevance to the subject matter (e.g., how the information in the documents would tend to prove or disprove some issue in the case); and (2) specific facts justifying discovery (e.g., why such information is necessary for trial preparation or to prevent surprise at trial.) Glenfed Develop. Corp. v. Sup. Ct. (National Union Fire Ins. Co. of Pittsburgh, Penn.) (1997) 53 Cal.App.4th 1113, 1117 (citations omitted); see also Kirkland v. Sup. Ct. (Guess? Inc.) (2002) 95 Cal.App.4th 92, 98. Declarations are generally used to show the requisite "good cause" for an order to compel inspection. The declarations must contain "specific facts" rather than mere conclusions. Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. v. Sup. Ct. (Paine Webber Real Estate Secur.) (1991) 233 Cal.App.3d 1138, 1141. Petitioners have not met their initial burden to show good cause in this motion. Where an agreement to comply has been made, but compliance is not forthcoming, the motion is to compel compliance pursuant to CCP Section 2031.320. Petitioners have not brought a motion to compel compliance. The Motion to Compel Gregory Crowe to: Provide a Complete and Legally Proper Privilege Log is GRANTED.
